mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand, which translates to higher corporate revenues and earnings, thereby driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the year-over-year growth rate of RSAFS shows no statistically significant positive correlation with S&P 500 20-day returns over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a deteriorating labor market, leading to reduced consumer confidence and spending, which negatively impacts corporate profits and S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of high consumer price levels (CPIAUCSL) and elevated federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) indicates a restrictive monetary policy environment aimed at curbing inflation, which typically dampens economic activity and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day returns are consistently positive when both CPIAUCSL is above 300 and FEDFUNDS is above 4.5%, or if the interaction term shows no predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts (HOUST) during an economic expansion signal robust investment and future economic activity, which provides a short-term boost to market sentiment and S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 5-day returns are negative or flat in periods of economic expansion when HOUST is above 1500, or if the signal performs poorly outside of expansionary regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals a contraction in manufacturing and overall supply-side weakness, which negatively impacts corporate revenues and S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day returns are positive or flat in more than 35% of instances where INDPRO declines by more than 1% over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent job creation (PAYEMS) reflects a healthy and growing labor market, supporting consumer confidence and spending, which in turn drives corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the month-over-month growth rate of PAYEMS shows no statistically significant positive correlation with S&P 500 20-day returns over any 7-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of high inflation (CPIAUCSL) and weak retail sales growth (RSAFS) indicates stagflationary pressures, where consumers face rising costs and reduced purchasing power, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day returns are positive during periods characterized by CPIAUCSL above 300 and negative retail sales growth, or if the interaction term lacks predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in GDP (GDPC1) for two consecutive quarters signals a broad economic contraction or recession, leading to severe negative market sentiment and S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 declines for two consecutive quarters
failure_condition: If S&P 500 5-day returns are positive or flat in more than 30% of instances following two consecutive quarters of GDPC1 decline during a declared recession.
